title: Dark Spirit
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Nickname}} {{Character |type=Dark Jedi |image=[[File:DarkSpirit-ESB.png]] |name=Dark Spirit |homeworld=[[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]]{{Film|V}} |birth= |death= |species=[[The Force/Legends|Force]] manifestation{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: Power of the Jedi|pack=Darth Vader (Dagobah)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/potjdagvader.asp}} |gender= |pronouns=new |height=2.03 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] {{C|in [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] form}} |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Dark side of the Force/Legends|Dark side of the Force]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|What's in there?''"
"''Only what you take with you.|Yoda and Luke Skywalker|}} A malevolent entity, the '''Dark Spirit''' was a powerful [[Metamorphosis|protean]] manifestation of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] that dwelt within the [[Cave of Evil/Legends|Dark Side Cave]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]]. In the years that followed the [[Declaration of a New Order|birth]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], a number of individuals confronted the Spirit. Those included the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], who faced an apparition of the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. Twenty-two years [[3 ABY/Legends|later]], the young [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] also [[Duel on Dagobah (3 ABY)/Legends|dueled]] the mysterious entity during his [[Jedi training/Legends|apprenticeship]] under Yoda, where the spirit assumed the form of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], his [[Parent/Legends|father]]. However Skywalker was able to defeat it and the apparition vanished once more. ==History== [[File:TheCave-SWGs7.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Dark Spirit takes the form of Dark Lord Darth Sidious.]] {{Quote|That which you seek, inside you will find.|Yoda to Galen Marek's clone|''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]''}} Around the year [[700 BBY]], a lone [[Bpfasshi/Legends|Bpfasshi]] [[Bpfasshi Dark Jedi leader|Dark Jedi who led]] a group of other [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] was [[Death/Legends|mortally]] wounded after a duel with the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Minch]]. The darksider was able to retreat into a cave on the [[Swamp/Legends|swamp]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]], where he took his last breath.{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?messageID=14866609#14882262|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-26|time=12:30 PM}} The site of the Dark Jedi's death absorbed their dark energies and became the [[Cave of Evil/Legends|Cave of Evil]] and came to home to a [[Metamorphosis|protean]] manifestation of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' known as the Dark Spirit. A fearful phantom was endowed with prophetic powers, and those who confronted it would learn about the darker side of their own nature. Although supposedly bound to the cave, the Dark Spirit could roam the swamp-covered landscape of Dagobah, radiating an aura of fear and hate that could cloud the minds of most [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]], thus weakening or even paralyzing them in the process. At least [[Dark Mara|one other entity]] with similar powers was known to have existed in the [[Dark Force Temple]] located on the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] planet of [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]].''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith]]'' In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the autocratic [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was [[Declaration of a New Order|formed]], and Yoda chose to settle on Dagobah in order to avoid the new government's [[Anti-Jedi sentiment/Legends|anti-Jedi]] persecutions.{{Film|III}} At some point, the old Jedi Master explored the Cave of Evil. He came to face the Dark Spirit, which had taken the form of his arch-foe [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] and [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]].{{Topps|set=2012 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 7|cardname=Beyond the Cave}} Immediately before his self-imposed [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] on Dagobah, Yoda had [[Skirmish in the Senate|fought]] the real Palpatine in the [[Grand Convocation Chamber]] of the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Rotunda]], and the Jedi had been forced to flee. Now in the Cave, Yoda confronted the grinning Spirit without [[Yoda's lightsaber/Legends|his]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], only armed with a [[Gimer stick/Legends|gimer stick]]. In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], the [[Subject 1138|clone]] of the late [[Galen Marek]], pioneer of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], came to Dagobah with the desire to find his identity. After entering the Cave of Evil, he encountered the Dark Spirit, who took several shapes. First, the entity appeared as several "clones" of the young man, all entangled in vines and asking for help. A little further on, the spirit transformed into the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Juno Eclipse/Legends|Juno Eclipse]], an old acquaintance of Marek, standing on the bridge of a ship. The clone then saw the false Eclipse getting attacked, which filled him with distress. He called out to the apparition, but both Eclipse and the rest of the vision faded all of a sudden. [[1 ABY/Legends|Two years later]], the young Force-sensitive [[Zak Arranda/Legends|Zak Arranda]] accidentally led a [[The Children|group]] of [[Diet/Legends|cannibal]] [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]] into the Cave. The Dark Spirit tortured them reenacting scenes of their past, when their [[Parent/Legends|parents]] were forced to nourish them upon their own flesh.''[[Galaxy of Fear: The Hunger]]'' [[File:Luke-trial.png|thumb|200px|Luke Skywalker battles himself as Darth Vader in the cave]] By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Yoda had been hiding from the Empire for [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. As part of his [[Jedi training/Legends|training]], Yoda sent the young [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] into the cave to [[Duel on Dagobah (3 ABY)/Legends|confront]] the Dark Spirit. Upon the apprentice's entry, the phantom took the form of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] and Palpatine's second-in-command. Unbeknownst to the young apprentice, Vader had once been a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] named Anakin Skywalker and was also his [[Parent/Legends|father]], whom Skywalker had been led to believe to have been murdered by Vader. After a short [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|duel]], Skywalker gave in to rage and [[Sai cha/Legends|beheaded]] the spirit. As the apprentice watched the [[Armor/Legends|helmet]]-encased head fall to the ground, the Dark Spirit's armored body completely vanished in the shadows.[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back'' novel]] For a moment the helmet remained on the ground completely still, then its faceplate exploded, revealing Luke Skywalker's own face, looking up at him. The young apprentice realized that, although that construct had been defeated, he still had much training left to do before confronting the real Vader. In no [[Time/Legends|time]], the ghostly vision of the decapitated head dissolved into nothingness. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|Help me! Help me!|The Spirit to Galen Marek's clone, in the form of several other clones|}} [[File:Dark_Spirit_TFU2.png|thumb|left|The Spirit could impersonate more than one individual at a time.]] The Dark Spirit was able take on the form of its intended victim's fears or darker sides. It could move unhindered and nearly undetected through the swamp of Dagobah, only being noticed by strong Force-sensitives. In battle, the entity radiated an aura of hate and fear that could unnerve and even paralyze an opponent. Unlike [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghosts]], which were normally impalpable,''[[Galaxy of Fear: Ghost of the Jedi]]'' the Spirit had all the appearances of tangibility for those who faced it. As Darth Vader, and Palpatine, it even used a phantom [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] that could cut like a genuine lightsaber. More than just morphing into a single being, the spirit could actually assume the shape of several individuals at a time and mimic entire scenes with a realistic background. It could also alter the vision that its victim had of their own appearance. ==Behind the scenes== In the games ''[[LEGO Star Wars II: The Original Trilogy]]''''[[LEGO Star Wars II: The Original Trilogy]]'' and ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'', the duel with the Dark Spirit is very different for gameplay reasons. For example, Yoda takes part in the duel because two players are required in all levels.''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' "''[[Game On!]]''", an article published in the [[Star Wars Insider 135|''Star Wars Insider'' 135]], cited the duel in the [[Cave of Evil/Legends|Cave of Evil]] with the Dark Spirit in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Empire Strikes Back'']] as a possible inspiration for the fake Vaders ([[Clados Vader|Clados]], [[Gyaos Vader|Gyaos]], [[Sasori Vader|Sasori]] and [[Wampa Vader|Wampa]]) in the ambiguously [[canon]] Japanese [[Star Wars (1987 video game)|''Star Wars'']] game from [[1987]], who were said to have been "intended as illusions representing Luke's fears."{{InsiderCite|135|Game On!}} However, since those game opponents were able to kidnap Luke Skywalker's allies and hold them prisoner, the game contradicts the article's statements, since they appeared to be real creatures rather than mere illusions.[[Star Wars (1987 video game)|''Star Wars'' video game (1987)]] In the [[Wikipedia:Game Boy Advance|Game Boy Advance]] game ''[[Star Wars Trilogy: Apprentice of the Force]]'', during the Dagobah: Training portion of the Empire Strikes Back storyline, the Dark Spirit in Vader's appearance is encountered as a boss. However, unlike in the film, the spirit doesn't actually do anything and only requires a lightsaber strike to bring him down, and as such his defeat animation only depicts him keeling down and moving his arm up and down. In addition, his defeat also omits his decapitation at the hands of Skywalker. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]'' *[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II'' novelization]] {{Ab|Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (audiobook)}} *''[[Galaxy of Fear: The Hunger]]'' *{{Film|V}} *[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back (junior novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'' junior novelization]] *[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back'' novelization]] {{Ab|Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *''[[The Life and Legend of Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]'' {{Ab|A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} ===Non-canon appearances=== *[[Star Wars Infinities: A New Hope 2|''Star Wars Infinities: A New Hope'' 2]] *''[[LEGO Star Wars II: The Original Trilogy]]'' *''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Complete Saga]]'' ==Sources== *''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'' {{Imo}} *{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Luke Feels the Dark Side|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC003.jpg}} *''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Mysteries of the Jedi]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Beware the Sith]]'' {{Po}} *{{Topps|set=2012 Topps Star Wars Galaxy Series 7|cardname=Beyond the Cave}} *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]'' {{Po}} *{{FFGTCG|set=Assault on Echo Base|cardname=Confronting the Terror|link=the-hoth-cycle/assault-on-echo-base/confronting-the-terror-assault-on-echo-base-53-5}} {{Po}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Dark side]] [[Category:Force entities]] [[Category:Impersonators]]
